mnemonics.title: Template Syntax
mnemonics.content: TEMPLATES: T-emplate E-xplicit M-embers P-rovide L-ogic A-nd T-ype E-xtensible S-olutions

mnemonics.title: SFINAE Rule
mnemonics.content: SFINAE: Substitution Failure Is Not An Error - when one template fails, try another instead of erroring

mnemonics.title: Template vs Overload Priority
mnemonics.content: Non-template functions have priority over template functions when both are equally good matches

acronyms.expansion: Substitution Failure Is Not An Error
acronyms.usage: Used in template metaprogramming to enable/disable template functions based on type traits
A compile-time polymorphism technique where a class inherits from a template parameter
Name lookup rule in C++ that also considers arguments' namespaces, important for template functions
A template parameter that is itself a template, like template<template<typename> class Container>

visualTips.visualization: Think of a template as a rubber stamp and instantiation as pressing it onto paper with ink - each press (call) creates a new concrete stamp (function) with the specific type
Like a puzzle piece fitting - the compiler tries to fit the provided arguments into the template's parameter slots to determine the missing pieces (types)
Template specialization is like a层层 cake - the most specific (specialized) version gets picked first, then general templates, then regular functions

analogies.analogy: A cookie cutter - one cutter shape (template) can make cookies of different flavors (types), but all have the same shape and structure
A restaurant menu - the general menu (primary template) applies to everyone, but special dishes (specialized versions) are available for specific dietary needs
A fixed-size container - like a suitcase of a specific dimension, where the size is known at compile time rather than runtime
A选择性过滤器 - when one filter fails, the system tries the next filter rather than crashing, like trying different sized sieves until one fits
